study_group_representative.quote: This study was performed to review the epidemiology of the confirmed COVID-19 cases reported from January to May 2020 and to assess the overall effectiveness of the various public health measures.

intervention_and_participants_defined.quote: Between January and May 2020, 1084 confirmed COVID-19 cases were reported... Various public health measures, including enhanced surveillance, border control, and social distancing, were introduced in phases in response to the prevailing local and global situations.

outcome_assessed_for_60pct.quote: A total of 1084 confirmed cases were reported between January and May 2020... All cases were isolated in public hospitals. As at 31 May 2020, 1036 patients had been discharged... Four deaths were reported

follow_up_length_reported.quote: We reviewed the case epidemiology and the various public health measures implemented from January to May 2020.

outcome_assessors_blinded.quote: Relevant epidemiological and clinical data collected from CHP epidemiological investigations were extracted from the CHP electronic databases.

outcome_definition_objective_precise.quote: A confirmed COVID-19 case was defined according to the case definition issued by the Centre for Health Protection (CHP)... confirmation required the detection (e.g., by reverse transcription polymerase chain reaction (RT-PCR)) of severe acute respiratory syndrome coronavirus 2 (SARS-CoV-2) in a clinical specimen.

important_prognostic_factors_accounted_f.quote: A total of 1084 confirmed cases were reported between January and May 2020, comprising 588 males and 496 females (male to female ratio, 1.19:1) who ranged in age from 40 d to 96 y (mean 37.5, median 35 y).

analysis_described_and_effect_quantified.quote: The epidemiological and clinical profiles of the confirmed cases were described and compared between different case classifications and phases of the epidemic, using appropriate statistical tests with Microsoft Excel 2013 and R version 3.6.0... Their overall effectiveness was assessed by calculating the 7-day moving averages of the daily number of confirmed cases reported and the interval from symptom onset to hospital admission (onset-to-admission interval)
